chapters.title: Organizational health is vital for every company that wishes to succeed
chapters.text:
Organizational health is a topic most leaders of firms are ignorant of; they prefer to work smart, often making rash decisions and causing a divide between members of staff. Organizational health is important because it relieves the firm from any in-house competition and gears all members of staff toward a common goal that favors the company at large.

If you wish to take your firm to an improved place ahead of the competition, you should invest in organizational health.

Effective methods exist to promote the overall success of an administrative unit and ensure the utmost health of an organization. These methods will, in turn, form the framework for the day-to-day running of the firm without any glitch or difficulty.

A successful leadership team shouldn’t contain more than ten and not less than three people. This way, the strength of the group directly affects the quality of the decisions made by it.

It is important to find a balance in the right amount of qualified members assigned to a team.

A good leadership team must create a system of vulnerability and trust within themselves to have full transparency amongst them. This is done so that no one is trying to outdo the other, and each decision reflects the collective mind of the group. Vulnerability can be achieved by sharing personal information within the group to build kinship and intimacy.

Without trust and vulnerability, no team is expected to succeed; trust is the glue that holds any group together.

Armed with the knowledge of welcomed vulnerability and healthy conflict resolution, the team can move to hold meetings with clearly laid out intentions and messages. There will be no room for misunderstanding. Each person leaves the meeting with a good grip on what was agreed upon, and the objective is shared by all that were present.

The following chapters give a breakdown of the advantages that a healthy organizational structure provides any firm. It is important that readers acquaint themselves with the steps to follow to achieve this healthy structure and implement them for effective results.

If people don’t weigh in, they can’t buy in. ~ Patrick Lencioni

Patrick Lencioni

chapters.title: A leadership team will excel if it values accountability, trust, and commitment
chapters.text:
A healthy and well-functioning team is aware of its responsibility to the general body. Each member is held responsible for their actions and behaviors because they have already instilled a sense of duty and accountability within their ranks. A group that actively adheres to its goals and commitments and holds each member accountable for a derailment will function smoothly—there isn’t just one person in charge of keeping check; everyone is keeping everyone on their toes.

Accountability in life and business ensures that everyone is responsible both for their actions and the actions of a team member.

A good team knows to place their individual goals aside and pool together resources first to achieve the collective goal of the team. The leadership team will succeed if it regards its separate goals and objectives as secondary to those of the group. This way, they can dedicate their full-time effort to the task required to complete the general objective.

But the objective can sometimes be unclear to everyone, and each member might be going about it the wrong way. This is where clarity cannot be overemphasized within a group that seeks to achieve a healthy organizational structure. Clarity here is being able to express the team’s objectives without any confusion from the recipients. Clarity makes sure that members of the team are well aware and passionate about the cause for putting the team together.

As leadership teams strengthen their bond, they are afforded the sixth sense of clarity due to the new ability to be fully open to all.

The attention to clarity also entails a set of rules and regulations that govern the behavior of the members. These are the dictates to be adhered to and also with which defaulters are to be punished by. It is an extension of accountability to obey the rules of the team you belong to. The leadership team should set an example by being willing first to follow the rules set to govern them and their actions.

No one on a cohesive team can say, Well, I did my job. Our failure isn’t my fault. ~ Patrick Lencioni

Patrick Lencioni

Did you know? The top companies have a healthy organizational structure and culture; examples are Twitter, Chevron, Google, etc.

chapters.title: Creating clarity aids the leadership team to obtain a better picture of what is expected of them
chapters.text:
Clarity helps greatly in the decision-making process. Once there is a lucid idea of what the organization stands for and what makes it different from the rest, it will stand out from its competition. Clarity in the direction the team wants to take the company is vital because it gives a plain path to avoid copying the competition’s tactics or goals. The trick is to be better than the norm, not to conform to it. When there is an understandable mission or target, there is a margin for success or failure for the team.

Without a clear goal, the greatest team would be useless.

Success or failure is heavily dependent on team members and their attitude toward teamwork. When each person is directly involved in the team’s success, there should be a sense of accountability also, as previously discussed. Each member can ask the other for a kind of progress report to ascertain the level of work each person has accomplished. The roles and the accompanying responsibilities assigned to each member of the group are thoroughly understood. The dutiful adherence to the individual roles and responsibilities will ensure a greater success rate of whatever goal was to be met.

Each team member is expected to check in on others to gauge the progress of the team—a way of gathering progress reports on the project at hand.

The degree to which the group’s roles, responsibilities, and goals as a collective are decided upon depends on the leadership team. There should be a check put on how much clarity is required to achieve a team objective to avoid revealing more information than is needed or risking exposing the organization’s secret to the public. This way, the team members aren’t burdened with too much information that might not be useful to the objective at hand.

Did you know? You can’t beat having team members who are pleasant and friendly to each other and are both good at and love what they are doing. No program, activity, or set of rules tops having happy and fulfilled employees who feel that what they are doing matters.

chapters.title: There should be clarity on the key expectations that are to be achieved
chapters.text:
Clarity should only entail disseminating the right amount of information that is beneficial to the organization. Leaders are to ensure the role of clarity, its importance, and the right way to implement it.

It is instilled into the minds of the departments that make up the team, who then must drill home the message to ensure there is no confusion as to what is expected of individuals and the group, with no misunderstanding. There should be a weekly or monthly briefing where updated memos are read aloud, and the team members are made fully aware of whatever new expectations they are to meet.

It is the responsibility of each member to instill the goals and values of the organization into their respective departments and units.

This clarity will translate into a greater understanding of the mission statement or vision of the organization for each person involved. An agreement will ensue as to what should be done and how to go about bringing to fruition the general goal of the body. Each member attending the meeting will be fully informed on what to do, how to do it, and why they must do it.

This ensures that the individual members of the team can accurately and confidently articulate the organization’s aim and objectives, its values, the strategies it will employ, and the goals these are centered toward achieving.

Clarity brings confidence, and when every member of a team understands, they are a lot more efficient and will get things done.

Often, employees drone away with an ambiguous set of expectations from the organization’s heads, but with clarity comes a better command of the actions a staff member can execute and a better grasp of its effect on the organization.

chapters.title: It is expensive to hire and then train members of staff on the company values
chapters.text:
If you wish to have members on your team who hold dear the values of the company as much as you do, you can start at the root and ensure that the recruits are all made to go through an extensive testing process. This process selects them based on their standings or understanding of what is expected of them concerning the company’s goals and values. This way, you don’t spend time and money training the already employed members of staff; you simply hire those who are already fit for the job and hold the values of the company dear to heart.

It will cost less for a company to hire people who fit the firm’s criteria instead of hiring anyone and training them to fit in with the firm’s objectives.

When these new intakes are given tasks and a clear goal to achieve, they are scored based on their performance. Their performances provide a better valuation of their readiness to embody the company’s values and culture.

Tests are a great way of gauging the strengths and weaknesses of your team, and they help you select the best from the crop of potentials.

Picking off the best performers does not spell doom for the poor performers, quite the contrary. If you notice a struggling member of your team from your testing, you nurture and coach them until they perform to optimal levels. Those who don’t meet the demands should not be discarded; they can benefit from practical training. Although they might not fit into the value system of the organization and would be a bad fit, they are coached regardless and given the right tools to succeed in the future.

If, after handpicking staff members carefully, some still struggle to meet up, it’s your duty to coach and guide them to reach their full potential.

Those who had performed admirably in the tests from earlier are to be rewarded based solely on the values and goals of the organization. When one member of the team achieves a certain organizational goal, he or she is to be compensated greatly. They serve as an incentive for good service, and people will perform better once they know their efforts will be recognized. Every human is prompted or inspired by the prospects of gratification for service.

chapters.title: A great way to foster the bond within a team is to engage in frequent meetings to strategize
chapters.text:
In the case of meetings that we discussed earlier, there should be different meetings for different discussions. Strategy meetings should not clash with assessment meetings. It is important not to clump the meetings together; information should be disseminated. There should be a clear distinction for each meeting arrangement. If there is to be a meeting for team confidence building, then communicate it and refrain from bringing up anything else.

Messages should be set up to avoid any information clash, meetings with specific messages.

In strategy meetings, new strategies are only discussed once the progress reports from the last meeting have been considered and appraised. The progress report is placed next to the goals initially set, and if they fall short, help should accompany each meeting, correction, and coaching to do better before the new strategy is suggested.

Instead of clumping multiple issues into one long meeting, let each pressing issue have its own meeting.

There should also be room for clarity about very pressing issues during these meetings. While every other topic has been lightly brushed over, it would be very beneficial to the group if greater care and import is placed on the major issues in the form of emphasis. This gives room for clarification in case anyone was confused about what they were meant to do. It encourages the members of the team to throw upon a debate on novel ways to achieve the stated plan and which strategies would be best. And finally, it leads to a healthy resolution of ideas and decisions best suited for the benefit of the collective.

A meeting should allow the team to debate issues, choose the best strategies to tackle them and arrive at a healthy conclusion.

Meetings aren’t meant to be held at the office exclusively; members are encouraged to hold quarterly meetings outside the office. The meeting location should be somewhere calm enough to discuss the important strategies without the pressure of the actual workplace.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
Organizational health is largely overlooked in firms today because of a lack of understanding, but it is the most significant advantage any company can possess. When a firm has a healthy structure, it has minimal in-house politics, minimal confusion, high morals, high productivity, and lower turnover.

A firm that wishes to excel in the industry and overcome its competition should prize the need for organizational health. This decision starts with setting up a team of leaders and not depending solely on the opinions and decisions of one person. As a team, it can be trusted to fine-tune and churn out only the best ideas and decisions that will benefit the firm.

Sometimes leaders believe that meetings are irrelevant and the role of deciding what to be done is solely up to them; this behavior is usually a precursor to downfall frоm time immemorial. If one is to succeed with a team, there must be several avenues to exchange ideas and have honest reviews on the progress or lack of it. A team that must win must work together, fluidly and honestly.

Without criticism, a team cannot truly improve or grow; there must be constant, sincere communication. It is the strength of the team that gives the business an advantage over others.

The most well-intentioned, well-designed departmental communication program will not tear down silos unless the people who created those silos want them torn down. ~ Patrick Lencioni

Patrick Lencioni

Try this

Employ members of staff that hold dear the firm’s values and are willing to drive home the organization’s goals. They will be great additions to the team unit. Recruit those who will keep the values as they will save you the stress of training them.

Read it till the end?
